metadata.title: Epic 4 Preparation - Process, Context, and Research

story.iWant: comprehensive Epic 4 preparation including process improvements, story regeneration, and technical research
story.soThat: Epic 4 (Multi-Tenancy) starts with fresh context, documented patterns, and validated prerequisites
story.tasks: - Phase 1: Process Improvements (Epic 2 Outstanding) - Add "Axon Testing Patterns" section to architecture.md - Create Story Definition of Done checklist - Phase 2: Continuous Context Process (Epic 3 Finding) - Document Continuous Story Context in CONTRIBUTING.md - Add continuous context to Story DoD checklist - Phase 3: Story Preparation (Critical Blockers) - Regenerate all 10 Epic 4 stories with current template - Generate Story Context XML for all 10 Epic 4 stories - Create batch regeneration automation script - Phase 4: Technical Research (Epic 4 Prerequisites) - Research Axon ThreadLocal Propagation Patterns - Setup LitmusKt Framework Early - PostgreSQL RLS Research & Documentation - Phase 5: Security Research (OWASP Preparation) - Document SSRF Protection requirements - Research Circuit Breaker patterns

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. All Epic 2 outstanding action items completed (2 items) 2. Continuous Story Context process documented and implemented 3. All 10 Epic 4 stories regenerated with current template format 4. Story Context XML generated for all 10 Epic 4 stories 5. Batch story regeneration automation created 6. Axon ThreadLocal propagation patterns researched and documented 7. LitmusKt framework setup completed for early concurrency testing 8. PostgreSQL RLS research completed (Elena knowledge transfer) 9. OWASP security research documented for future stories 10. All preparation work validated and tested

doc.title: Epic 3 Retrospective - Authentication & Authorization
doc.snippet: Story Context XML created at epic start became stale by Stories 3.7-3.10. Stories worked with outdated patterns from 3.1-3.3, missing patterns from 3.4-3.6. Impact: Story 3.7 multiple iterations, Story 3.10 9.5h investigation. Solution: Continuous Story Context regeneration after each story.

doc.title: Epic 3 Retrospective
doc.snippet: PR #92 identified critical gaps in supply chain security (65/100) and exception handling (60/100) that must be addressed BEFORE Epic 4 to prevent tenant isolation failures. PR was too large (13K additions, 44 files) with build failures.

doc.title: Epic 2 Retrospective - Walking Skeleton
doc.snippet: Two action items remain from Epic 2: (1) Document Axon Testing Patterns (mode=subscribing, @ServiceConnection timing, Container as @Bean, PropagatingErrorHandler, aggregate caching), (2) Create Story Definition of Done checklist with quality gates.

doc.title: EAF Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Epic 4 implements 3-layer defense-in-depth tenant isolation: Layer 1 (JWT → ThreadLocal), Layer 2 (Command validation), Layer 3 (PostgreSQL RLS). Includes async context propagation, cross-tenant leak detection, per-tenant quotas, and LitmusKt concurrency testing. Fail-closed security: missing tenant context immediately rejects requests.

doc.title: EAF Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: ThreadLocal-based tenant context storage with stack-based management, WeakReference for memory safety, thread isolation validation, and cleanup after request completion.

doc.title: EAF Epic Breakdown
doc.section: Story 4.5: Tenant Context Propagation to Async Event Processors
doc.snippet: AxonTenantInterceptor propagates tenant_id to async event processors via event metadata. Critical for projection updates and event handlers to maintain tenant context in asynchronous processing.

doc.title: EAF Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: RLS policies enforce tenant isolation at database level using current_setting('app.tenant_id'). Even SQL injection or bugs cannot breach tenant boundaries. Performance target: <2ms overhead per query.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: 3-layer tenant isolation architecture with fail-closed design. TenantContext uses ThreadLocal for request-scoped storage. Axon interceptors for command validation and async propagation. PostgreSQL RLS as final defense layer.

artifact.reason: Existing security infrastructure from Epic 3 provides JWT validation patterns that Epic 4 will extend for tenant_id extraction
Axon Framework configuration base for implementing ThreadLocal propagation interceptors in Phase 4
Existing test infrastructure demonstrates Kotest, Testcontainers, and Nullable Pattern usage from Epic 2-3

story-context.constraints: - Story 4.0 is a PREPARATION story, not an implementation story - All work is documentation, research, and process improvement - NO production code changes except documentation updates - Phase 3 uses existing create-story workflow in #yolo mode - Phase 3 uses existing story-context workflow for all 10 Epic 4 stories - LitmusKt setup in Phase 4 must NOT interfere with Epic 4 story scope (extract from Story 4.10) - PostgreSQL RLS research in Phase 4 includes Elena knowledge transfer (pair programming) - Batch regeneration script must be reusable for Epic 5, 6, 7+ - All documentation in English (document_output_language from config) - Continuous Story Context process must prevent Epic 3 context drift repetition

interface.name: Story Template Format
interface.signature: Stories must include: Status, Story, ACs, Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Notes, Dev Agent Record (Context Reference, Model Used, Debug Log, Completion Notes, File List, Change Log)

tests.standards: All testing follows Constitutional TDD (test-first mandatory), 7-Layer Defense (static, unit, integration, property, fuzz, concurrency, mutation), Kotest 6.0.4 framework (JUnit forbidden), Testcontainers for stateful dependencies, Nullable Design Pattern for stateless infrastructure. Story 4.0 has NO production code, therefore NO new tests required. Validation is via documentation review and workflow execution verification.
tests.locations: - framework/*/src/test/kotlin (framework module tests) - products/*/src/test/kotlin (product module tests) - shared/testing/src/main/kotlin (Nullable implementations and test utilities)

ideas.idea: Verify Epic 2 action items completed by reading architecture.md for Axon Testing Patterns section and checking for Story DoD checklist file
Verify Continuous Story Context process documented in CONTRIBUTING.md with workflow reminder/automation guidance
Verify all 10 Epic 4 stories regenerated by checking story file format includes Tasks/Subtasks, Dev Agent Record sections
Verify Story Context XML exists for all 10 Epic 4 stories (4.1-4.10) in docs/sprint-artifacts/epic-4/*.context.xml
Verify batch regeneration script exists at scripts/regenerate-epic-stories.sh with error handling and progress reporting
Verify Axon ThreadLocal propagation patterns documented in architecture.md Multi-Tenancy section with MessageHandlerInterceptor examples
Verify LitmusKt dependency added to framework/multi-tenancy test dependencies with example concurrency test for TenantContext
Verify PostgreSQL RLS research documented in architecture.md with current_setting() pattern and widget_view table examples
Verify OWASP SSRF protection documented based on A01:2025 and Circuit Breaker patterns researched for Epic 5+
Verify all preparation work complete via checklist validation and documentation review
